An online mathematical encyclopedia devoted to Deriving and exploring mathematics beyond the classroom for high school students. All Things Are Derivable

Our mission is to make mathematical reasoning transparent, tangible, and telling through Derivation while offering additional pathways for inquisitive and motivated students to explore mathematics with greter rigor and depth.
This project aims to demystify mathematical Deriving and to foster deeper curiosity through clear and careful reasoning.

Throughout this project, Derivation is treated as a proper noun, referring to the process of obtain a result through meticulous, step-by-step calculation. As the main theme of Art of Derivation, all words related to Derivation (such as Derive, Deriving) are highlighted in the main theme color. Some closely related terms, though not strictly equivalent, including Proof, Justification, and Logical Argument, all of which are integral to the idea of Derivation presented in this project. My understanding of Derivation was mainly developed from my AP Physics C: Mechanics course, where the Free Response Questions frequently require student to Derive expressions from fundamental definitions or equations in the provided equations sheet.
This project is built on Docusaurus and deployed on GitHub Pages. Below are detailed format information.

All documents are stored as Markdown files and use either the .md or .mdx extension. All document file names are capitalized and connected using hyphens (-). All documents are stored under the docs directory, organized by sidebars.js. All referenced images (such as .jpg, .png, .svg) are stored in the static/img directory.
All documents start with the front matter to ensure the consistent format and navigation. More additional details, refer to Markdown Features | Docusaurus.
All mathematical expressions are rendered using KaTeX with remark-math and follow standard LaTeX\LaTeXLATEвЂ‹X syntax.

All Derivation processes are placed in collapsible blocks, implemented using <details>+<summary>. All Derivation processes are organized using the align* enviornment. All Derivation processes are completed with \blacksquare (в– \blacksquareв– ), indicating Q.E.D. (quod erat demonstrandum), meaning "that which was to be demonstrated".

Note The Gray Adomination provides extra and unnecessary notes and information. Definition The Green Adomination provides definition about the given topic. Property, Identity, Solution... The Blue Adomination provides Properties, Identities, Solutions, and so on that is NOT designed to show the collapsable Derivation process. Derivation The collapsable Adomination provides detailed process for Derivation Warning The Orange Adomination provides information that is important to know.

The theme color of this project is Indigo (#4b0082) in light mode and Magenta (#ff61ff) in dark mode. The theme color is exclusively reserved for Derivation and its related concepts, rather than decorative styling.
The idea of the favicon came from в€µ\becauseв€µ ($\because$) and в€ґ\thereforeв€ґ ($\therefore$).

All documents are organized in a logical sequence; following the given order is strongly recommended. Before viewing the full derivation through the collapsable bar, attempt to work through each step independently using pen and paper. Detailed solutions are provided in the collapsible sections for verification and deeper understanding. Maintaining a dedicated notebook for your own derivations is highly encouraged.

This page lists several fundamental integrals and their basic properties. Their Derivations are mostly straightforward, as they follow directly from reversing those common derivatives.

This page will explain Integral in a nut shell,

Below are (30/147) advanced integral formulas listed in the appendix of the Higher Mathematics (Tongji University). I will Derive each formula step by step. It may seem futile and impractival to memorize all the formulas, but it is a great opportunity to consolidate integration techniques and appreciate heuristics behind them.

In my AP Physics C Mechanics class, many students were struggling to Derive the terminal velocity of a free-falling object, and the primary reason was that they can't solve the Integral Formula #21. My teacher back then found a really sophisticated way through hyperbolic trigonometry substitution, and everyone was stunned by the complexity, but I came up with a simple algebraic transformation. Substitution is the most common integral technique in elementary calculus. Recall what we mentioned in common integrals that we use uuu and du\mathrm{d}udu instead of xxx and dx\mathrm{d}xdx, and this is because uuu may represent an entire function, not just a single variable. We will tackle with this more specifically in this section.

We will start with this integral question: в€«3x2sinвЃЎx3 dx= ?\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\ ?в€«3x2sinx3 dx= ? Note that there is an product, which is beyond all common integrals, so we need to simplify to an easier form. An example is below: Solution Let: u=x3u=x^3u=x3 dudx=3x2du=3x2 dx\begin{align*} \frac{\mathrm{d}u}{\mathrm{d}x}&=3x^2\\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}u}&=\color{red}{3x^2\ \mathrm{d}x}\\ \end{align*}dxduвЂ‹duвЂ‹=3x2=3x2 dxвЂ‹ Plug in back to the question: в€«3x2sinвЃЎx3 dx=в€«sinвЃЎu du=в€«sinвЃЎu du=в€’cosвЃЎu+C=в€’cosвЃЎx3+C\begin{align*} \int{\color{red}{3x^2}}\sin{x^3}\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}x}&=\int{\sin{u}}\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}u}\\ &=\int{\sin{u}}\ \mathrm{d}u\\ &=-\cos{u}+C\\ &=-\cos{x^3}+C \end{align*}в€«3x2sinx3 dxвЂ‹=в€«sinu du=в€«sinu du=в€’cosu+C=в€’cosx3+CвЂ‹ Hence: в€«3x2sinвЃЎx3 dx=в€’cosвЃЎx3+Cв– \int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=-\cos{x^3}+C \tag*{$\blacksquare$}в€«3x2sinx3 dx=в€’cosx3+Cв–  By replacing the variable xxx into a function of uuu, we can simplify the overall question to a common integral. As you can see, this is a method we inversely use the Chain Rule, and generally, it is called: The substitution rule Given a differenciable function u=g(x)u=g(x)u=g(x), and fff is continuous on its interval, then: в€«f(g(x))gвЂІ(x) dx=в€«f(u) du\int{f\left(g\left(x\right)\right)g'(x)}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int{f\left(u\right)}\ \mathrm{d}uв€«f(g(x))gвЂІ(x) dx=в€«f(u) du Definite Integral In the case of Definite Integral, the bound should also changes, as: в€«abf(g(x))gвЂІ(x) dx=в€«g(a)g(b)f(u) du\int_{a}^{b}{f\left(g\left(x\right)\right)g'(x)}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int_{g(a)}^{g(b)}{f\left(u\right)}\ \mathrm{d}uв€«abвЂ‹f(g(x))gвЂІ(x) dx=в€«g(a)g(b)вЂ‹f(u) du

Direct Substitution is a method created by the author himself. It simplify the process of substitution without write out uuu and du\mathrm{d}udu. This time, let's try what I called Direct Substituton: Direct Substitution Approach After analyze, we want to substitute x3x^3x3. All we need to do is substitude dx\mathrm{d}xdx to a fraction where the top is dx3\mathrm{d}x^3dx3 and the bottom is the derivative of x3x^3x3 based on xxx, as: в€«3x2sinвЃЎx3 dx=в€«3x2sinвЃЎx3 d(x3)3x2=в€«sinвЃЎx3 d(x3)\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int{\cancel{3x^2}\sin{x^3}}\ \frac{\mathrm{d}(x^3)}{\cancel{3x^2}}=\int{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)в€«3x2sinx3 dx=в€«3x2sinx3 3x2d(x3)вЂ‹=в€«sinx3 d(x3) And all we need is to consider x3x^3x3 as uuu and integrate normally: вџ№ в€«sinвЃЎx3 d(x3)=в€’cosвЃЎx3+C\implies \int{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)=-\cos{x^3}+Cвџ№в€«sinx3 d(x3)=в€’cosx3+C More importantly, for definite integral, it doesn't need to change bounds, as: в€«ab3x2sinвЃЎx3 dx=в€«absinвЃЎx3 d(x3)=в€’cosвЃЎx3в€Јab=в€’cosвЃЎb3+cosвЃЎa3\int_{a}^{b}3x^2\sin{x^3}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int_{a}^{b}{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)=-\cos{x^3}\big|_{a}^{b}=-\cos{b^3}+\cos{a^3}в€«abвЂ‹3x2sinx3 dx=в€«abвЂ‹sinx3 d(x3)=в€’cosx3вЂ‹abвЂ‹=в€’cosb3+cosa3 Direct Substitution In general, if we want to substitude ax+bax+bax+b or term that can cancel the other one, we may use direct substitution в€«f(x)g(x)вЏџSubstitude dx=в€«f(x)g(x) d(g(x))gвЂІ(x)\int{{f(x)}\underbrace{g(x)}_{\text{Substitude}}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int{f(x)g(x)}\ \frac{\mathrm{d}(g(x))}{g'(x)} в€«f(x)Substitudeg(x)вЂ‹вЂ‹ dx=в€«f(x)g(x) gвЂІ(x)d(g(x))вЂ‹

Menelaus's Theorem Menelaus's theorem is named for Greek Mathematician Menelaus of Alexandria that said: given a line and a в–іABC\triangle ABCв–іABC, if the line cross through the sides of в–іABC\triangle ABCв–іABC ABABAB, ACACAC, and BCBCBC or their extension at point LLL, MMM, and NNN as shown in graph, then: AMвЂѕMBвЂѕв‹…BLвЂѕLCвЂѕв‹…CNвЂѕNAвЂѕ=1 \frac{\overline{AM}}{\overline{MB}}\cdot \frac{\overline{BL}}{\overline{LC}} \cdot \frac{\overline{CN}}{\overline{NA}}=1MBAMвЂ‹в‹…LCBLвЂ‹в‹…NACNвЂ‹=1 where AMвЂѕ\overline{AM}AM represents the length of segment AMAMAM Derivation Derivation 1: Areas Connect ALALAL, as shown on the left. Since there are some triangles share the same base and altitude: AMвЂѕMBвЂѕ=[в–іALM][в–іBLM],BLвЂѕLCвЂѕ=[в–іBLM][в–іCLM],CNвЂѕNAвЂѕ=[в–іCLM][в–іALM] \frac{\overline{AM}}{\overline{MB}}=\frac{[\triangle ALM]}{[\triangle BLM]}, \frac{\overline{BL}}{\overline{LC}}=\frac{[\triangle BLM]}{[\triangle CLM]}, \frac{\overline{CN}}{\overline{NA}}=\frac{[\triangle CLM]}{[\triangle ALM]}MBAMвЂ‹=[в–іBLM][в–іALM]вЂ‹,LCBLвЂ‹=[в–іCLM][в–іBLM]вЂ‹,NACNвЂ‹=[в–іALM][в–іCLM]вЂ‹ Hence: AMвЂѕMBвЂѕв‹…BLвЂѕLCвЂѕв‹…CNвЂѕNAвЂѕ=[в–іALM][в–іBLM]в‹…[в–іBLM][в–іCLM]в‹…[в–іCLM][в–іALM]=1в–  \frac{\overline{AM}}{\overline{MB}}\cdot \frac{\overline{BL}}{\overline{LC}} \cdot \frac{\overline{CN}}{\overline{NA}}=\frac{[\triangle ALM]}{[\triangle BLM]}\cdot \frac{[\triangle BLM]}{[\triangle CLM]} \cdot \frac{[\triangle CLM]}{[\triangle ALM]} =1 \tag*{$\blacksquare$}MBAMвЂ‹в‹…LCBLвЂ‹в‹…NACNвЂ‹=[в–іBLM][в–іALM]вЂ‹в‹…[в–іCLM][в–іBLM]вЂ‹в‹…[в–іALM][в–іCLM]вЂ‹=1в–  Note: [в–іAOD][\triangle AOD][в–іAOD] represents the area of в–іAOD\triangle AODв–іAOD Derivation 2: Trigonometry Let в€ AMN=в€ BML=О±\angle AMN=\angle BML=\alphaв€ AMN=в€ BML=О±, в€ ANM=ОІ\angle ANM=\betaв€ ANM=ОІ, в€ BLM=Оі\angle BLM=\gammaв€ BLM=Оі. sinвЃЎ(О±в€’ОІ)=sinвЃЎО±cosвЃЎОІв€’cosвЃЎО±sinвЃЎОІ\sin{\left(\alpha-\beta\right)}=\sin{\alpha}\cos{\beta}-\cos{\alpha}\sin{\beta}sin(О±в€’ОІ)=sinО±cosОІв€’cosО±sinОІ Derivation sinвЃЎОІ=BCAB=BC1 вџ№ BC=sinвЃЎОІcosвЃЎОІ=ACAB=AC1 вџ№ AC=cosвЃЎОІsinвЃЎО±=ADAC=ADcosвЃЎОІ вџ№ AD=sinвЃЎО±cosвЃЎОІcosвЃЎО±=BFBC=BFsinвЃЎОІ вџ№ BF=cosвЃЎО±sinвЃЎОІ \begin{align*} \blue{\sin{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AB}=\frac{BC}{1}\implies BC&=\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \blue{\cos{\beta}}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\sin{\alpha}}=\frac{AD}{AC}=\frac{AD}{\blue{\cos{\beta}}}\implies AD&=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\cos{\alpha}}=\frac{BF}{BC}=\frac{BF}{\blue{\sin{\beta}}}\implies BF&=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \end{align*}sinОІ=ABBCвЂ‹=1BCвЂ‹вџ№BCcosОІ=ABACвЂ‹=1ACвЂ‹вџ№ACsinО±=ACADвЂ‹=cosОІADвЂ‹вџ№ADcosО±=BCBFвЂ‹=sinОІBFвЂ‹вџ№BFвЂ‹=sinОІ=cosОІ=sinО±cosОІ=cosО±sinОІвЂ‹sinвЃЎ(О±в€’ОІ)=BGAB=BG1=AE=ADв€’ED=ADв€’BF=sinвЃЎО±cosвЃЎОІв€’cosвЃЎО±sinвЃЎОІ \begin{align*} \sin{(\green{\alpha}-\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{BG}{AB}=\frac{BG}{1}=AE\\ &=AD-ED\\ &=AD-BF\\ &=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}-\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}sin(О±в€’ОІ)вЂ‹=ABBGвЂ‹=1BGвЂ‹=AE=ADв€’ED=ADв€’BF=sinО±cosОІв€’cosО±sinОІвЂ‹в– вЂ‹

cosвЃЎ(О±+ОІ)=cosвЃЎО±cosвЃЎОІв€’sinвЃЎО±sinвЃЎОІ\cos{\left(\alpha+\beta\right)}=\cos{\alpha}\cos{\beta}-\sin{\alpha}\sin{\beta}cos(О±+ОІ)=cosО±cosОІв€’sinО±sinОІ Derivation sinвЃЎОІ=BCAB=BC1 вџ№ BC=sinвЃЎОІcosвЃЎОІ=ACAB=AC1 вџ№ AC=cosвЃЎОІsinвЃЎО±=BFAC=BFsinвЃЎОІ вџ№ BF=sinвЃЎО±sinвЃЎОІcosвЃЎО±=ADBC=ADcosвЃЎОІ вџ№ AD=cosвЃЎО±cosвЃЎОІ \begin{align*} \blue{\sin{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AB}=\frac{BC}{1}\implies BC&=\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \blue{\cos{\beta}}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\sin{\alpha}}=\frac{BF}{AC}=\frac{BF}{\blue{\sin{\beta}}}\implies BF&=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \green{\cos{\alpha}}=\frac{AD}{BC}=\frac{AD}{\blue{\cos{\beta}}}\implies AD&=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \end{align*}sinОІ=ABBCвЂ‹=1BCвЂ‹вџ№BCcosОІ=ABACвЂ‹=1ACвЂ‹вџ№ACsinО±=ACBFвЂ‹=sinОІBFвЂ‹вџ№BFcosО±=BCADвЂ‹=cosОІADвЂ‹вџ№ADвЂ‹=sinОІ=cosОІ=sinО±sinОІ=cosО±cosОІвЂ‹cosвЃЎ(О±+ОІ)=AEAB=AE1=ADв€’ED=ADв€’BF=cosвЃЎО±cosвЃЎОІв€’sinвЃЎО±sinвЃЎОІ \begin{align*} \cos{(\green{\alpha}+\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{AE}{AB}=\frac{AE}{1}\\ &=AD-ED\\ &=AD-BF\\ &=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}-\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}cos(О±+ОІ)вЂ‹=ABAEвЂ‹=1AEвЂ‹=ADв€’ED=ADв€’BF=cosО±cosОІв€’sinО±sinОІвЂ‹в– вЂ‹

cosвЃЎ(О±в€’ОІ)=cosвЃЎО±cosвЃЎОІ+sinвЃЎО±sinвЃЎОІ\cos{\left(\alpha-\beta\right)}=\cos{\alpha}\cos{\beta}+\sin{\alpha}\sin{\beta}cos(О±в€’ОІ)=cosО±cosОІ+sinО±sinОІ Derivation sinвЃЎОІ=BCAB=BC1 вџ№ BC=sinвЃЎОІcosвЃЎОІ=ACAB=AC1 вџ№ AC=cosвЃЎОІsinвЃЎО±=CFBC=CFsinвЃЎОІ вџ№ CF=sinвЃЎО±sinвЃЎОІcosвЃЎО±=CDAC=CDcosвЃЎОІ вџ№ CD=cosвЃЎО±cosвЃЎОІ \begin{align*} \blue{\sin{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AB}=\frac{BC}{1}\implies BC&=\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \blue{\cos{\beta}}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\sin{\alpha}}=\frac{CF}{BC}=\frac{CF}{\blue{\sin{\beta}}}\implies CF&=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \green{\cos{\alpha}}=\frac{CD}{AC}=\frac{CD}{\blue{\cos{\beta}}}\implies CD&=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \end{align*}sinОІ=ABBCвЂ‹=1BCвЂ‹вџ№BCcosОІ=ABACвЂ‹=1ACвЂ‹вџ№ACsinО±=BCCFвЂ‹=sinОІCFвЂ‹вџ№CFcosО±=ACCDвЂ‹=cosОІCDвЂ‹вџ№CDвЂ‹=sinОІ=cosОІ=sinО±sinОІ=cosО±cosОІвЂ‹cosвЃЎ(О±в€’ОІ)=AGAB=AG1=DF=CD+CF=cosвЃЎО±cosвЃЎОІ+sinвЃЎО±sinвЃЎОІ \begin{align*} \cos{(\green{\alpha}-\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{AG}{AB}=\frac{AG}{1}=DF\\ &=CD+CF\\ &=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}+\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}cos(О±в€’ОІ)вЂ‹=ABAGвЂ‹=1AGвЂ‹=DF=CD+CF=cosО±cosОІ+sinО±sinОІвЂ‹в– вЂ‹

tanвЃЎ(О±+ОІ)=tanвЃЎО±+tanвЃЎОІ1в€’tanвЃЎО±tanвЃЎОІ\tan{\left(\alpha+\beta\right)}=\frac{\tan{\alpha}+\tan{\beta}}{1-\tan{\alpha}\tan{\beta}}tan(О±+ОІ)=1в€’tanО±tanОІtanО±+tanОІвЂ‹ Derivation tanвЃЎО±=CDAD=CD1 вџ№ CD=tanвЃЎО±secвЃЎО±=ACAD=AC1 вџ№ AC=secвЃЎО±tanвЃЎОІ=BCAC=BCsecвЃЎО± вџ№ BC=secвЃЎО±tanвЃЎОІsecвЃЎО±=BCCF=secвЃЎО±tanвЃЎОІCF вџ№ CF=tanвЃЎОІtanвЃЎО±=BFCF=BFtanвЃЎОІ вџ№ BF=tanвЃЎО±tanвЃЎОІ \begin{align*} \green{\tan{\alpha}}=\frac{CD}{AD}=\frac{CD}{1}\implies CD&=\green{\tan{\alpha}}\\ \green{\sec{\alpha}}=\frac{AC}{AD}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\green{\sec{\alpha}}\\ \blue{\tan{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AC}=\frac{BC}{\green{\sec{\alpha}}}\implies BC&=\green{\sec{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}\\ \green{\sec{\alpha}}=\frac{BC}{CF}=\frac{\green{\sec{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}}{CF}\implies CF&=\blue{\tan{\beta}}\\ \green{\tan{\alpha}}=\frac{BF}{CF}=\frac{BF}{\blue{\tan{\beta}}}\implies BF&=\green{\tan{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}} \end{align*}tanО±=ADCDвЂ‹=1CDвЂ‹вџ№CDsecО±=ADACвЂ‹=1ACвЂ‹вџ№ACtanОІ=ACBCвЂ‹=secО±BCвЂ‹вџ№BCsecО±=CFBCвЂ‹=CFsecО±tanОІвЂ‹вџ№CFtanО±=CFBFвЂ‹=tanОІBFвЂ‹вџ№BFвЂ‹=tanО±=secО±=secО±tanОІ=tanОІ=tanО±tanОІвЂ‹tanвЃЎ(О±+ОІ)=BEAE=DFAE=CD+CFADв€’BF=tanвЃЎО±+tanвЃЎОІ1в€’tanвЃЎО±tanвЃЎОІ \begin{align*} \tan{(\green{\alpha}+\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{BE}{AE}=\frac{DF}{AE}=\frac{CD+CF}{AD-BF}\\ &=\frac{\green{\tan{\alpha}}+\blue{\tan{\beta}}}{1-\green{\tan{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}tan(О±+ОІ)вЂ‹=AEBEвЂ‹=AEDFвЂ‹=ADв€’BFCD+CFвЂ‹=1в€’tanО±tanОІtanО±+tanОІвЂ‹вЂ‹в– вЂ‹

tanвЃЎ(О±в€’ОІ)=tanвЃЎО±в€’tanвЃЎОІ1+tanвЃЎО±tanвЃЎОІ\tan{\left(\alpha-\beta\right)}=\frac{\tan{\alpha}-\tan{\beta}}{1+\tan{\alpha}\tan{\beta}}tan(О±в€’ОІ)=1+tanО±tanОІtanО±в€’tanОІвЂ‹ Derivation tanвЃЎО±=ADCD=AD1 вџ№ AD=tanвЃЎО±secвЃЎО±=ACCD=AC1 вџ№ AC=secвЃЎО±tanвЃЎОІ=BCAC=BCsecвЃЎО± вџ№ BC=secвЃЎО±tanвЃЎОІsecвЃЎО±=BCBF=secвЃЎО±tanвЃЎОІBF вџ№ BF=tanвЃЎОІtanвЃЎО±=CFBF=CFtanвЃЎОІ вџ№ CF=tanвЃЎО±tanвЃЎОІ \begin{align*} \green{\tan{\alpha}}=\frac{AD}{CD}=\frac{AD}{1}\implies AD&=\green{\tan{\alpha}}\\ \green{\sec{\alpha}}=\frac{AC}{CD}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\green{\sec{\alpha}}\\ \blue{\tan{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AC}=\frac{BC}{\green{\sec{\alpha}}}\implies BC&=\green{\sec{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}\\ \green{\sec{\alpha}}=\frac{BC}{BF}=\frac{\green{\sec{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}}{BF}\implies BF&=\blue{\tan{\beta}}\\ \green{\tan{\alpha}}=\frac{CF}{BF}=\frac{CF}{\blue{\tan{\beta}}}\implies CF&=\green{\tan{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}} \end{align*}tanО±=CDADвЂ‹=1ADвЂ‹вџ№ADsecО±=CDACвЂ‹=1ACвЂ‹вџ№ACtanОІ=ACBCвЂ‹=secО±BCвЂ‹вџ№BCsecО±=BFBCвЂ‹=BFsecО±tanОІвЂ‹вџ№BFtanО±=BFCFвЂ‹=tanОІCFвЂ‹вџ№CFвЂ‹=tanО±=secО±=secО±tanОІ=tanОІ=tanО±tanОІвЂ‹tanвЃЎ(О±в€’ОІ)=BGAG=AEDF=ADв€’BFCD+CF=tanвЃЎО±в€’tanвЃЎОІ1+tanвЃЎО±tanвЃЎОІ \begin{align*} \tan{(\green{\alpha}-\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{BG}{AG}=\frac{AE}{DF}=\frac{AD-BF}{CD+CF}\\ &=\frac{\green{\tan{\alpha}}-\blue{\tan{\beta}}}{1+\green{\tan{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}tan(О±в€’ОІ)вЂ‹=AGBGвЂ‹=DFAEвЂ‹=CD+CFADв€’BFвЂ‹=1+tanО±tanОІtanО±в€’tanОІвЂ‹вЂ‹в– вЂ‹

sinвЃЎ2О±=2sinвЃЎО±cosвЃЎО±\sin{2\alpha}=2\sin{\alpha}\cos{\alpha}sin2О±=2sinО±cosО± Derivation sinвЃЎ2О±=sinвЃЎ(О±+О±)=sinвЃЎО±cosвЃЎО±+cosвЃЎО±sinвЃЎО±(Trig Identity #4)=2sinвЃЎО±cosвЃЎО±\begin{align*} \sin{2\alpha}&=\sin{({\alpha}+{\alpha})}\\ &=\sin{{\alpha}}\cos{{\alpha}}+\cos{{\alpha}}\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#4}})\\ &=2\sin{\alpha}\cos{\alpha} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}sin2О±вЂ‹=sin(О±+О±)=sinО±cosО±+cosО±sinО±=2sinО±cosО±вЂ‹вЂ‹(Trig Identity #4)вЂ‹в– вЂ‹ Trig Identity #4

cosвЃЎ2О±=cosвЃЎ2О±в€’sinвЃЎ2О±=2cosвЃЎ2О±в€’1=1в€’2sinвЃЎ2О±\cos{2\alpha}=\cos^2{\alpha}-\sin^2{\alpha}=2\cos^2{\alpha}-1=1-2\sin^2{\alpha}cos2О±=cos2О±в€’sin2О±=2cos2О±в€’1=1в€’2sin2О± Derivation cosвЃЎ2О±=cosвЃЎ(О±+О±)=cosвЃЎО±cosвЃЎО±в€’sinвЃЎО±sinвЃЎО±(Trig Identity #6)=cosвЃЎ2О±в€’sinвЃЎ2О±=cosвЃЎ2О±в€’(1в€’cosвЃЎ2О±)=2cosвЃЎ2О±в€’1(Trig Identity #1)=(1в€’sinвЃЎ2О±)в€’sinвЃЎ2О±=1в€’2sinвЃЎ2О±(Trig Identity #1)\begin{align*} \cos{2\alpha}&=\cos{({\alpha}+{\alpha})}\\ &=\cos{{\alpha}}\cos{{\alpha}}-\sin{{\alpha}}\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#6}})\\ &=\cos^2{\alpha}-\sin^2{\alpha}\\ &=\cos^2{\alpha}-(1-\cos^2{\alpha})=2\cos^2{\alpha}-1 &&({\text{Trig Identity \#1}})\\ &=(1-\sin^2{\alpha})-\sin^2{\alpha}=1-2\sin^2{\alpha}&&({\text{Trig Identity \#1}}) \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}cos2О±вЂ‹=cos(О±+О±)=cosО±cosО±в€’sinО±sinО±=cos2О±в€’sin2О±=cos2О±в€’(1в€’cos2О±)=2cos2О±в€’1=(1в€’sin2О±)в€’sin2О±=1в€’2sin2О±вЂ‹вЂ‹(Trig Identity #6)(Trig Identity #1)(Trig Identity #1)вЂ‹в– вЂ‹ Trig Identity #6 Trig Identity #1

sinвЃЎ3О±=в€’4sinвЃЎ3О±+3sinвЃЎО±\sin{3\alpha}=-4\sin^3{\alpha}+3\sin{\alpha}sin3О±=в€’4sin3О±+3sinО± Derivation sinвЃЎ3О±=sinвЃЎ(2О±+О±)=sinвЃЎ2О±cosвЃЎО±+cosвЃЎ2О±sinвЃЎО±(Trig Identity #4)=2sinвЃЎО±cosвЃЎО±cosвЃЎО±+cosвЃЎ2О±sinвЃЎО±(Trig Identity #10)=2sinвЃЎО±(cosвЃЎ2О±)+(1в€’2sinвЃЎ2О±)sinвЃЎО±(Trig Identity #11)=2sinвЃЎО±(1в€’sinвЃЎ2О±)+(1в€’2sinвЃЎ2О±)sinвЃЎО±(Trig Identity #1)=2sinвЃЎО±в€’2sinвЃЎ3О±+sinвЃЎО±в€’2sinвЃЎ3О±=в€’4sinвЃЎ3О±+3sinвЃЎО±\begin{align*} \sin{3\alpha}&=\sin{({2\alpha}+{\alpha})}\\ &=\sin{{2\alpha}}\cos{{\alpha}}+\cos{{2\alpha}}\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#4}})\\ &=2\sin{\alpha}\cos{\alpha}\cos{\alpha}+\cos{{2\alpha}}\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#10}})\\ &=2\sin{\alpha}(\cos^2{\alpha})+(1-2\sin^2{\alpha})\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#11}})\\ &=2\sin{\alpha}(1-\sin^2{\alpha})+(1-2\sin^2{\alpha})\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#1}})\\ &=2\sin{\alpha}-2\sin^3{\alpha}+\sin{\alpha}-2\sin^3{\alpha}\\ &=-4\sin^3{\alpha}+3\sin{\alpha} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}sin3О±вЂ‹=sin(2О±+О±)=sin2О±cosО±+cos2О±sinО±=2sinО±cosО±cosО±+cos2О±sinО±=2sinО±(cos2О±)+(1в€’2sin2О±)sinО±=2sinО±(1в€’sin2О±)+(1в€’2sin2О±)sinО±=2sinО±в€’2sin3О±+sinО±в€’2sin3О±=в€’4sin3О±+3sinО±вЂ‹вЂ‹(Trig Identity #4)(Trig Identity #10)(Trig Identity #11)(Trig Identity #1)вЂ‹в– вЂ‹ Trig Identity #4 \quad Trig Identity #10 Trig Identity #11 \quad Trig Identity #1

cosвЃЎ3О±=4cosвЃЎ3О±в€’3cosвЃЎО±\cos{3\alpha}=4\cos^3{\alpha}-3\cos{\alpha}cos3О±=4cos3О±в€’3cosО± Derivation cosвЃЎ3О±=cosвЃЎ(2О±+О±)=cosвЃЎ2О±cosвЃЎО±в€’sinвЃЎ2О±sinвЃЎО±(Trig Identity #6)=(2cosвЃЎ2О±в€’1)cosвЃЎО±в€’sinвЃЎ2О±sinвЃЎО±(Trig Identity #11)=2cosвЃЎ3О±в€’cosвЃЎО±в€’2sinвЃЎО±cosвЃЎО±sinвЃЎО±(Trig Identity #10)=2cosвЃЎ3О±в€’cosвЃЎО±в€’2cosвЃЎО±(1в€’cosвЃЎ2О±)(Trig Identity #1)=2cosвЃЎ3О±в€’cosвЃЎО±в€’2cosвЃЎО±+2cosвЃЎ2О±=4cosвЃЎ3О±в€’3cosвЃЎО±\begin{align*} \cos{3\alpha}&=\cos{({2\alpha}+{\alpha})}\\ &=\cos{{2\alpha}}\cos{{\alpha}}-\sin{{2\alpha}}\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#6}})\\ &=(2\cos^2{\alpha}-1)\cos{\alpha}-\sin{{2\alpha}}\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#11}})\\ &=2\cos^3{\alpha}-\cos{\alpha}-2\sin{\alpha}\cos{\alpha}\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#10}})\\ &=2\cos^3{\alpha}-\cos{\alpha}-2\cos{\alpha}(1-\cos^2{\alpha}) &&({\text{Trig Identity \#1}})\\ &=2\cos^3{\alpha}-\cos{\alpha}-2\cos{\alpha}+2\cos^2{\alpha}\\ &=4\cos^3{\alpha}-3\cos{\alpha} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}cos3О±вЂ‹=cos(2О±+О±)=cos2О±cosО±в€’sin2О±sinО±=(2cos2О±в€’1)cosО±в€’sin2О±sinО±=2cos3О±в€’cosО±в€’2sinО±cosО±sinО±=2cos3О±в€’cosО±в€’2cosО±(1в€’cos2О±)=2cos3О±в€’cosО±в€’2cosО±+2cos2О±=4cos3О±в€’3cosО±вЂ‹вЂ‹(Trig Identity #6)(Trig Identity #11)(Trig Identity #10)(Trig Identity #1)вЂ‹в– вЂ‹ Trig Identity #6 \quad Trig Identity #11 Trig Identity #11 \quad Trig Identity #1
Let's discover Docusaurus in less than 5 minutes.
Get started by creating a new site. Or try Docusaurus immediately with docusaurus.new.
Node.js version 20.0 or above: When installing Node.js, you are recommended to check all checkboxes related to dependencies.

Generate a new Docusaurus site using the classic template. The classic template will automatically be added to your project after you run the command: npm init docusaurus@latest my-website classic You can type this command into Command Prompt, Powershell, Terminal, or any other integrated terminal of your code editor. The command also installs all necessary dependencies you need to run Docusaurus.

Run the development server: cd my-website npm run start The cd command changes the directory you're working with. In order to work with your newly created Docusaurus site, you'll need to navigate the terminal there. The npm run start command builds your website locally and serves it through a development server, ready for you to view at http://localhost:3000/. Open docs/intro.md (this page) and edit some lines: the site reloads automatically and displays your changes.
